title: centaurea × nouletiana
intro: Centaurea × nouletiana, also known as New York aster, is a hybrid plant species within the daisy family, derived from the cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea montana. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centaurea × nouletiana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Centaurea × nouletiana can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it accessible for use in herbal preparations such as teas, salves, and ointments.
